summary: Full backend + frontend testing of DreamWedds async render queue + bundled music library. All 12 pytest backend tests passed (health, music list, music streaming for all 3 tracks, 404 handling, async render returns jobId in <10s, status transitions queued->rendering with fractional progress ->done with progress=1.0, video mp4 download, list includes completed job, unknown musicId returns 400, nonexistent render 404). Frontend e2e passed: MusicPicker renders 4 options (No music + serenity + twilight + marigold-bloom), preview buttons render, selection highlights correctly. Full render flow via UI: queued -> rendering (observed progress 0%, 28%, 40%, 57%, 87%) -> rendered-video-player + download-video-btn visible. Note: duration-select has options 10/20/30 seconds (not 5s as mentioned in request); tests used 10s successfully.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py `_run_render_job` references `logger` inside the except block, but `logger` is defined AFTER the function at line 322 (via basicConfig). This works because the reference is lazy at call-time and module-level `logger` exists by then, but it is fragile — move logging setup and logger definition to top of file.

MUSIC_LIBRARY 'duration' field (32s) does not match actual mp3 length; if music must sync with 5s render there is no trimming logic — track just gets whatever Remotion does. Consider documenting or trimming server-side.
server.py list_renders defaults status to 'done' (line 273) — if a Mongo doc is missing the field it will be labeled done. Better default to 'unknown' or 'queued'.
Duration options in UI (10/20/30) don't align with review request expectation of 5s; consider adding 5s option for quick testing.
PreviewPane 'download-video-btn' uses `<a download>` with cross-origin video URL — browsers may ignore the download attribute and navigate instead. Consider fetching blob then triggering download.
seed_data_creation: One async render job persisted in Mongo db.renders (id 41d401d5...) plus test-created uploads.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Async render for durationInSeconds=5 finishes in ~15-20s including polling. Radix Select 'duration-select' requires click then get_by_role('option', name='10 seconds (quick test)'). Only 10/20/30 duration options exist. MusicPicker uses 4 buttons: music-option-none + music-option-{id}, and separate music-select-{id}/music-preview-{id} inside each track card. All backend/api routes prefixed /api. Render service internal at :4001.
